teacher_notes: This week we worked on fractions, narrative writing, and started our science unit on living things.
generated_content:
Dear families,

This week in Year 3 Blue, we have been busy exploring fractions in Mathematics. The children learned to recognise and represent unit fractions like halves, quarters, and thirds using real objects and diagrams. They practised sharing collections equally and connecting fractions to division.

In English, we began our narrative writing unit. Students are learning to structure stories with a clear beginning, middle, and end, using descriptive language to bring their characters to life. In Science, we kicked off our Living Things classification unit, where students observed and grouped animals by their features. It has been a wonderful week of learning!
home_activities: Cut a sandwich or pizza into equal parts and ask your child to name the fractions (half, quarter, third)
Ask your child to write a short story about their weekend using a beginning, middle, and end structure
Go on a nature walk and classify the living things you find — insects, plants, birds
guided_prompts: Has your child talked about fractions at home this week?
What story characters has your child been creating in their writing?
What living things did your child find most interesting to classify?

teacher_notes: Multiplication facts, persuasive writing, and heat transfer experiment
generated_content:
Dear families,

This week in Mathematics, we focused on building fluency with multiplication facts up to 10x10. Students used arrays, skip counting, and games to practise recalling facts quickly.

In English, we started persuasive writing. Students are learning to form opinions, support them with reasons, and use persuasive language. Our topic was screen time — the debates were very lively! In Science, we conducted a heat transfer experiment, testing which materials keep water warm the longest. The children recorded their observations carefully.
home_activities: Play times tables games together — try dice multiplication or Hit the Button online
Have a family debate about a topic your child cares about, encouraging them to give reasons
Test which cup keeps hot chocolate warm longest — plastic, ceramic, or metal
guided_prompts: Which multiplication facts does your child find easiest and hardest?
What topic did your child choose for their persuasive writing?
Did your child share what they learned about heat transfer?
